---
id: BUG-022
title: promotion integration attempts collide on effect_key for already-integrated packets
depends_on: []
write_set: ["src/promotion/**","src/promotion/promotion.test.ts","src/promotion/promotion.test.support.types.ts","playbook.config.json","docs/packets/**"]

---

## Problem

The promotion controller computes the integration-attempt effect key as digest({ targetRef, beforeSha, candidateSha }). When two distinct packets reach the already-integrated close path from the same target SHA, they share the same effect key, causing a UNIQUE constraint failure on the second promotion.

## Task

Include the packet task id in the integration-attempt effect key so that each packet's promotion is uniquely identifiable.

## Required behavior

- promotion_integration_attempts.effect_key is unique per (taskId, targetRef, beforeSha, candidateSha).
- Existing already-integrated promotions remain readable; the change only affects future keys.
- A regression test proves two distinct packets can be promoted from the same already-integrated candidate SHA.

## RED test

After the fix, a RED test demonstrates that promoting two already-integrated packets from the same candidate SHA no longer collides on effect_key.

## Stop conditions

- Do not change promotion semantics.
- Do not modify paths outside the packet write set.

## Evidence

- final-sha
- clean-verify-receipt
- regression-test-receipt
